← Lucid MDM

Lucid MDM · worldpop · match or refuse

250 countries. Five publishers. One golden record each.

5 public sources publish the world’s countries under 5 different naming regimes — Türkiye, Turkey, Turkiye. A deterministic, name-only matcher clustered their rows into golden records; every surviving field carries its provenance, and where the publishers disagree the store keeps every cited value — a TENSION, never an average. Type a country name below: the same normalization and integer-cosine trigram rules run in your browser, and if nothing clears the matcher’s line, the engine refuses. No server, no model, no network.

3,416source rows in
250golden records
1,026TENSIONs kept
14CONTRADICTIONs kept
98.91% / 98.29%matcher precision / recall (machine alone, pre-steward)
6steward merges
b013add5d2b8…store fingerprint

Stewardship — where the machine honestly stopped

The name-only matcher refused to merge these 6 pairs — honestly: no normalization or trigram rule connects “Somalia” to “Somalia, Fed. Rep.” without also inventing merges elsewhere. A steward decided them instead. Each decision is a declared, HMAC-signed receipt in the store’s ledger (actor steward:demo-curation); replaying the receipts over the matcher’s 257 clusters reproduces the 250 shipped goldens. The precision/recall above (98.91% / 98.29%) is the machine alone, measured BEFORE these merges — the steward’s work is added on top, never laundered into the matcher’s score.

SOM-qualifierpublisher-qualifier

World Bank styles the country 'Somalia, Fed. Rep.'; every other source says 'Somalia'. Same entity, publisher styling.

joined: datasets_population:SOM geonames:51537 mledoze:SOM owid:SOM wb_pop:SOM

TUR-renameofficial-rename

'Turkey' -> 'Türkiye' (official UN name change, 2022). GeoNames/OWID still publish the pre-rename form.

joined: datasets_population:TUR geonames:298795 mledoze:TUR owid:TUR wb_pop:TUR

CPV-renameofficial-rename

'Cape Verde' -> 'Cabo Verde' (official rename, 2013). Sources straddle the rename.

joined: datasets_population:CPV geonames:3374766 mledoze:CPV owid:CPV wb_pop:CPV

event 6db783563afa2efc4a115ffe56aa2bb8b4a9f9fda9b1ddc634754dc41aaa4ca4

PSE-naming-disputenaming-dispute

'West Bank and Gaza' (World Bank), 'Palestinian Territory' (GeoNames) and 'Palestine' (mledoze/OWID) describe the same entity in these datasets; the naming is contested, the population series are not.

joined: datasets_population:PSE geonames:6254930 mledoze:PSE owid:PSE wb_pop:PSE

event 5a673b8319cd9d247bbbb3ac9a1489f6d3dd25042c550f9df9f6bf722573203b

historical flagkept, never merged

'Serbia and Montenegro' is a defunct entity (2003-2006) still present in the GeoNames feed. Kept as its own record, marked historical, never merged into Serbia or Montenegro.

record: geonames:8505033

The exemplar tension

14,077,269

people of honest disagreement — both cited, neither averaged.

China, 2021: 1,412,360,000 — datasets_population (CHN), wb_pop (CHN)  vs  1,426,437,269 — owid (CHN). The golden record carries every estimate with its witnesses; no synthetic “consensus” number exists anywhere in the store.

Honest scope. A proof, not a product. Matching is by name only (normalized token-sort, joined-form and trigram integer-cosine); the ISO3 ground truth is used only in the evaluation harness, never by the matcher. No figure here is claimed to be the “true” population — the store carries what the sources said, who said it, and where they disagree. Serbia and Montenegro (defunct 2003–2006) is kept as its own record, marked historical, never merged into Serbia or Montenegro. Storage is tamper-evident, never tamper-proof.

Store fingerprint (SHA-256 over the canonical store): b013add5d2b8ec5fd2949e15f9b532201d495b3a00c876db8d30c384222f95ae
